observations.recommendation:
a. We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned municipal officials/employees to immediately submit their liquidation reports and related documents or refund their cash advances.


b. We also recommended and the Management agreed that Accountable Officers ensure that all cash advances are liquidated within the specified period after the purpose of its grant has been served and/or before the end of each year.
We recommended and the Management agreed to stop the practice of charging to the LDRRMF expenses which are not enumerated in the aforesaid circular.

We recommended and the Management agreed to observe the provisions embodied in the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicles.


We also recommended and the Management agreed to prepare and properly fill up the forms and reports prescribed in the manual.

1.5	We recommended and the Accounting Office agreed to provide complete and correct details of the current and future PPE acquisitions for reference in the computation of depreciation and thereafter, ensure that depreciation is correctly computed and presented in the financial statements.
1.6	We also recommended and the Municipality agreed to conduct the one-time cleansing of its PPE balances in accordance with COA Circular 2020-006 to ensure that all PPE recorded in the books and financial statements are existing and duly supported.

2.8	We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to annually conduct a physical count of its PPE and immediately prepare and submit its corresponding RPCPPE.
2.9	We also recommended and the Inventory Committee and Accounting Office agreed to conduct the one-time cleansing of its PPE account balances, reconciling the same with property and accounting records in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular 2020-006.

3.6	We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the concerned municipal officials/employees to immediately submit their liquidation reports and related documents or refund their cash advances.
3.7	We also recommended and the Accountable Officers agreed to ensure that all cash advances are liquidated within the specified period after the purpose of their grant has been served and/or before the end of each year.
4.11	We recommended and Management agreed to stop the practice of charging to the   LDRRMF expenses which are not enumerated in the aforesaid circular.

5.6	We recommended and the Human Resource Management Office responsible personnel agreed to submit within five working days from execution/issuance the JOCs and their supporting documents for review by the Auditor.

We recommended and Management agreed to observe the provisions embodied in the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicles by submitting regularly the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and related supporting documents and reports.

a. We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Local Development Council and concerned offices/departments/officials to ensure the feasibility and timely implementation/completion of 20 percent development projects, especially development projects from continuing development funds, since the socio-economic benefits to be derived from them have already been deferred.

2.6	We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned municipal officials/employees to immediately submit their liquidation reports and related documents or refund their cash advances.
2.7	We also recommended and the Management agreed that Accountable Officers ensure that all cash advances are liquidated within the specified period after the purpose of its grant has been served and/or before the end of each year.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Local Development Council and concerned offices/departments/officials to ensure the feasibility and timely implementation/completion of 20 percent development projects, especially development projects from continuing development funds, since the socio-economic benefits to be derived from them have already been deferred.
We recommended and the Management agreed to stop the practice of charging to the LDRRMF expenses which are not enumerated in the aforesaid circular.

We recommended and the Management agreed to observe the provisions embodied in the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Vehicles.
We also recommended and the Management agreed to prepare and properly fill up the forms and reports prescribed in the manual.

We recommended and the Management agreed to exert means for the transfer of the title in the name of the Municipality and to appropriate funds for the titling of the properties. 

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to maintain complete accounting records and keep on file complete records of all the real properties.

We recommended and the management agreed that payments for the communication expenses, as well as other operating expenses of the Municipality be made directly to the suppliers in compliance with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184.

We recommended and the Management agreed that 
Stop the practice of charging to the LDRRMF expenses which are not enumerated in the aforesaid circular and those which do not support disaster risk management activities 


Maximize the utilization of the allocated fund for disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the General Services Officer or responsible personnel to submit within five working days from execution/issuance all Purchase Orders and their supporting documents for review by the Auditor.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct all the Department Heads/employees concerned to submit their detailed action plans on the audit observations and recommendations including that of prior years, and the information gathered therein be used to properly accomplish the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation, which should then be submitted to COA.

We also recommended that henceforth, the LGU of Jordan shall, within 60 days from receipt of the Annual Audit Report, submit to COA, either in printed form or by way of electronic document, a status report on the actions taken on said audit observations and recommendations.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer agreed to: a. Reconcile and update their records and ensure that PPELCs and PCs are maintained for all PPEs, and henceforth, make the necessary adjustments on any discrepancies noted; b. Exhaust all efforts to determine and obtain information on the above mentioned 13 PPE accounts with no details so that the same will be included in the physical count; and c. Prepare and submit the RPCPPE using the prescribed format in Annex A. Further, we also recommended that Management avail the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided under COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to establish PPE balances that that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to: a. Verify/ check the amounts in liquidation reports submitted to the Provincial Accountant of fund transfers with discrepancies in the balance recorded in the books of the Province; b. Determine the status of liquidations of unsettled fund transfers reflected in the books of the Province that has been fully liquidated in the books of the Municipality, and if necessary, provide the Provincial Accountant of copies of liquidation documents that have been submitted so that the unrecorded settlement will be taken up in the books of the Province; c. Cause the reconciliation of the inter-agency payable account Due to LGUs of the Municipality with that of the reciprocal receivable account Due from LGUs of the Province, and effect the necessary adjusting entries on the variance noted to reflect the correct balances of said accounts; and d. Conduct a periodic reconciliation of inter-agency reciprocal accounts to ensure accurate balances of payable/receivable accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of PPE in accordance with the guidelines prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006, to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability, as well as to ensure timely submission of the RPCPPE to the Auditor (following the required format attached in Annex A).

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
Create an Appraisal Committee to appraise the value of the PPEs whose records could no longer be recovered, and provide an estimate of the remaining useful lives of these assets to serve as bases of the Municipal Accountant in the computation of their depreciation expenses using the straight-line method; and
The recently completed projects be immediately provided with depreciation expenses using the straight-line method.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the HRMO to ensure that only the accumulated vacation leave credits of officials and employees are used for regular monetization and a maximum of 30 days is allowed to be monetized in a given year. In addition, proof of valid and justifiable reasons to avail of special monetization shall be attached in the applications for leave.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Local Development Council and concerned offices/departments/officials to:
1) Resolve the issues concerning the area and location of the projects to hasten the implementation and completion of the remaining 2021 non-implemented development projects; and
2) Carefully plan the projects that should be included in the Annual Development Plan/Annual Investment Program to ensure that the same are feasible for implementation within timeline.
We recommended and the Municipal DRRM Officer agreed to submit the required report to COA and strictly adhere to the provisions on submission of reports under COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that COVID-19 fund is utilized for programs and activities related to pandemic response and control and observe strict adherence to the prohibition of the Local Government Code against the utilization of fund other than for its intended purpose.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to exert efforts to retrieve the records pertaining to the PPEs with no details and provide the corresponding depreciation expenses of all PPEs with no depreciation, using the determined estimated useful lives and straight-line method of depreciation.

We recommended and Management agreed to: a. Cause the preparation of the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) by the Property Officer/Unit to facilitate the disposal of unserviceable properties; and b. Direct the Disposal Committee to expedite the disposal of unserviceable properties following the procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 92-386, to prevent further deterioration and to realize income therefrom if found to be saleable.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that brand names and complete detailed item specifications of the items procured are indicated in the Purchase Orders, in compliance with COA Circular No. 96-010.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Local Development Council and concerned offices/departments/officials to: a. Hasten the implementation and completion of the unimplemented CY 2020 development projects; and b. Thoroughly plan the projects that should be included in the Annual Development Plan/ Annual Investment Program and ensure that the same are feasible for implementation within the timeline.

We recommended and Management agreed to: 1. Require the Municipal Accountant to: a. Take up in the appropriate inventory account the cost of supplies, materials and relief goods procured for distribution, and in the corresponding expense account the subsequent issuance/distribution duly supported by the RIS and RSMI; b. Record in the books the inventory of welfare goods for distribution in custody of the Municipality based on the inventory report prepared; and c. Prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger Card for each inventory of welfare/ relief goods procured by the Municipality. 2. Require the Supply/ Property Officer to: a. Coordinate with the LDRRM Officer and MSWD Officer to prepare and submit an Inventory List/Report of Welfare Goods for Distribution in custody of the Municipality as of December 31, 2020 in the prescribed format in Annex E; and b. Prepare and maintain Stock Cards, Requisition and Issue Slip, and Report of Supplies and Materials Issued for welfare goods.

We recommended and Management agreed to: a. Cause the inclusion of a warranty clause in succeeding Contracts/ POs under Emergency Procurement of the Bayanihan Act, to ensure assurance of replacement of/ recovery on goods delivered with defects, and/ or do not conform to quality standards and technical specifications of the contract; and b. Ensure that all Contracts/ POs contain complete detailed item specifications pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996, to protect the interest of the government.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer agreed to:
Reconcile and update their records and  ensure that PPELCs and PCs are maintained for all PPEs, and henceforth, make the necessary adjustments on any discrepancies noted;
Exhaust all efforts to determine and obtain information on the above mentioned 13 PPE accounts with no details so that the  same will be included in the physical count; and
Prepare and submit the RPCPPE using the prescribed format in Annex A.

Further, we also recommended that Management avail the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided under COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to establish PPE balances that that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to exert efforts to retrieve the records pertaining to the PPEs with no details and provide the corresponding depreciation expenses of all PPEs with no depreciation, using the determined estimated useful lives and straight-line method of depreciation.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Cause the preparation of the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) by the Property Officer/Unit to facilitate the disposal of unserviceable properties; and
Direct the Disposal Committee to expedite the disposal of unserviceable properties following the procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 92-386, to prevent further deterioration and to realize income therefrom if found to be saleable.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that brand names and complete detailed item specifications of the items procured are indicated in the Purchase Orders, in compliance with COA Circular No. 96-010.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Local Development Council and concerned offices/departments/officials to:
Hasten the implementation and completion of the unimplemented CY 2020 development projects; and
Thoroughly plan the projects that should be included in the Annual Development Plan/Annual Investment Program and ensure that the same are feasible for implementation within the timeline.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the Municipal Accountant to:
Take up in the appropriate inventory account the cost of supplies, materials and relief goods procured for distribution, and in the corresponding expense account the subsequent issuance/distribution duly supported by the RIS and RSMI;
Record in the books the inventory of welfare goods for distribution in custody of the Municipality based on the inventory report prepared; and
Prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger Card for each inventory of welfare/relief goods procured by the Municipality.
Require the Supply/Property Officer to:
Coordinate with the LDRRM Officer and MSWD Officer to prepare and submit an Inventory List/Report of Welfare Goods for Distribution in custody of the Municipality as of December 31, 2020 in the prescribed format in Annex E; and
Prepare and maintain Stock Cards, Requisition and Issue Slip, and Report of Supplies and Materials Issued for welfare goods.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a.   Cause the inclusion of a warranty clause in succeeding Contracts/POs under Emergency Procurement of the Bayanihan Act, to ensure assurance of replacement of/recovery on goods delivered  with defects, and/or do not conform to quality standards and technical specifications of the contract; and
b. 	Ensure that all Contracts/POs contain complete detailed item specifications pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996, to protect the interest of the government.

We recommended and Management agreed that an Appraisal Committee be immediately created to provide information on the fair market value and remaining useful lives of the PPEs so that the Municipal Accountant could compute for the depreciation expenses of all depreciable assets, in accordance with the provisions of Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to: 
provide the Provincial Accountant with a copy of Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) on real property tax and other tax collections on a quarterly basis;  
submit details of the recorded balances of fund transfers for those payables recorded in 2016 and prior years; and  
follow up recording of liquidation reports to ensure that these are recorded by the Provincial Accountant and immediately submit liquidation documents for those unliquidated fund transfers recorded as Due from LGUs in the Province’s books.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

establish the road right-of-way for the Jordan Memorial Park immediately to facilitate the development of the new cemetery;         
require the implementing offices to expedite the completion of the above-mentioned development projects; and
direct the Municipal Development Council and the implementing offices to ensure that only projects which are feasible and can be completed within the year are included in the Annual Development Plan.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Inventory Committee prioritize the preparation of the complete 2019 RPCPPE. The report shall include all physically counted properties of the Municipality and categorized per PPE type, pursuant to the above mentioned prescribed guidelines and shall be immediately submitted to COA.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
the Municipal Accountant compute the depreciation expenses of these PPE accounts, using the determined estimated useful lives and straight-line method;
the Appraisal Committee appraise all the PPEs acquired previously, estimating the current fair market value and the remaining useful lives of these assets, as bases in the computation of the depreciation expenses; and
the Municipal Accountant shall then draw Journal Entry Voucher to record the current fair market values, the disposal of unserviceable and fully-depreciated PPEs and the depreciation expenses.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Regularly provide data to the Provincial Accountant on the Province’s share in the real property and other tax collections made by the Municipality and ensure reconciliation of the balances at year-end;
Submit details of the recorded balances of fund transfers for those payables recorded in prior years; and,
Follow up the recording of liquidation reports to ensure that these are recorded by the Provincial Accountant and immediately submit liquidation documents for those unliquidated fund transfers recorded as Due from LGUs in the Province's books.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that all POs include the brand names of the items being procured, in compliance with COA Circular No. 96-010. It was also recommended that the Inspectorate Committee and the end-user ensure that goods delivered are as specified/ indicated in the POs/Contracts.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to prepare and maintain cashbooks for Cash in Bank, by bank account for all funds. The cashbooks shall be updated daily and must be reconciled with the accounting records at the end of the month. Necessary corrections, if any, shall be effected immediately, to reconcile the cashbook and the accounting records.

We recommended that Management strictly enforce the provisions specified in the GAA on the net take home pay, to promote efficiency and effectiveness in public service by ensuring that government employees have enough to support their daily needs.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the implementing offices to expedite the completion of the above-mentioned development projects. It is also recommended that, for the succeeding years, the Local Sanggunian promptly approve the Annual Development Program to avoid delay in the implementation of the identified development programs and projects.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LDRRM Council and the Municipal DRRM Officer ensure that the annual LDRRMFIP include the projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years, and the submission of the required reports to COA, as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002 be strictly complied.
We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to prioritize the preparation and submission of the RPCPPE for CY 2018 as prescribed in the above mentioned guidelines.
We recommended and the Disposal Committee agreed to expedite the disposal of unserviceable properties following the procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 92-386.
We recommended and Management agreed to increase the amount of bond of the Special Disbursing Officer to ensure the maximum coverage of accountability.
We recommended and Management agreed to submit the copies of contracts within the prescribed period in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 for timely review thereof.

We recommended and Management agreed to implement the planned programs and projects within the approved implementation schedule so the constituents can timely receive the intended benefits thereof. In addition, we recommended the establishment of a Project Monitoring Team to oversee the entire project, from the formulation of plans to the implementation. It is necessary that there is a team to monitor the progress and resolve any problem that deters the timely completion of the projects.

We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to prioritize the preparation and submission of the RPCPPE for CY 2018 as prescribed in the above mentioned guidelines.
We also recommended that an additional personnel be assigned to assist the Inventory Committee in the preparation of documents and reports of inventory of the municipal properties.

We recommended and Management agreed that an Appraisal Committee be immediately created to provide information on the fair market value and remaining useful lives of the PPEs so that the Municipal Accountant could compute for the depreciation expenses of all depreciable assets, in accordance with the provisions of Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards.
We recommended and the Disposal Committee agreed to expedite the disposal of unserviceable properties following the procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 92-386.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
provide the Provincial Accountant with a copy of Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) on real property tax and other tax collections on a quarterly basis;
submit details of the recorded balances of fund transfers for those payables recorded in 2016 and prior years; and,
follow up recording of liquidation reports to ensure that these are recorded by the Provincial Accountant and immediately submit liquidation documents for those unliquidated fund transfers recorded as Due from LGUs in the Province’s books.
We recommended and Management agreed to increase the amount of bond of the Special Disbursing Officer to ensure the maximum coverage of accountability.
We recommended and Management agreed to submit the copies of contracts within the prescribed period in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 for timely review thereof.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
establish the road right-of-way for the Jordan Memorial Park immediately to facilitate the development of the new cemetery;
require the implementing offices to expedite the completion of the above-mentioned development projects; and,
direct the Municipal Development Council and the implementing offices to ensure that only projects which are feasible and can be completed within the year are included in the Annual Development Plan.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Engineer commented that several projects not implemented in CY 2018 were already on-going in 2019.

We recommended and Management agreed to implement the planned programs and projects within the approved implementation schedule so the constituents can timely receive the intended benefits thereof.
In addition, we recommended the creation of a Project Monitoring Team to oversee and monitor the progress of the entire project, from formulation of plans to its implementation, and resolve any problem that deters the project’s timely completion.

We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to:
validate all records available by conducting actual physical count per department;

coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer in reconciling the RPCPPE with the accounting records; and,

subsequently, furnish the Municipal Accountant with a copy of reconciled RPCPPE.

We recommended and the Management agreed that:
the specific estimated useful life for each PPE be determined based on the LGU’s experience on the life of its PPE; and

the Municipal Accountant to compute the depreciation expenses using the determined estimated useful lives and record the same in the books.

We recommended and Management agreed that a Disposal Committee be immediately reinstituted to expedite the disposal of the unserviceable properties to prevent their further deterioration and reduction of salable value resulting to additional loss for the Municipality. In addition, these unserviceable properties be turned over to the Property Officer and their corresponding Memorandum of Receipt or Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment be cancelled.

We recommended and Management agreed that demand letters be sent to the Accountable Officers with unliquidated cash advances and no additional cash advances be granted to them without liquidation of the previous ones. Further, the salaries of the AOs with long-overdue cash advances be withheld until the same are settled/liquidated.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

require the Municipal Engineer to ensure that the projects are implemented  in accordance with the approved project implementation schedule and ADM guidelines; and

ensure that the projects’ documentary requirements as required in the DBM-DILG JMC 2017-3 be promptly prepared and completed before the receipt of ADM funds.

We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to:
validate all records available by conducting actual physical count per department;
coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer in reconciling the RPCPPE with the accounting records; and,
subsequently, furnish the Municipal Accountant with a copy of reconciled RPCPPE.

We recommended and the Management agreed that:
the specific estimated useful life for each PPE be determined based on the LGU’s experience on the life of its PPE; and
the Municipal Accountant to compute the depreciation expenses using the determined estimated useful lives and record the same in the books.
It was further recommended that an Appraisal Committee then be created to assess the current fair market value and remaining useful lives of the PPEs which shall be used as the basis of the Municipal Accountant in the computation of depreciation expenses.

We recommended and Management agreed that a Disposal Committee be immediately reinstituted to expedite the disposal of the unserviceable properties to prevent their further deterioration and reduction of salable value resulting to additional loss for the Municipality. In addition, these unserviceable properties be turned over to the Property Officer and their corresponding Memorandum of Receipt or Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment be cancelled.

We recommended and Management agreed that demand letters be sent to the Accountable Officers with unliquidated cash advances and no additional cash advances be granted to them without liquidation of the previous ones. Further, the salaries of the AOs with long-overdue cash advances be withheld until the same are settled/liquidated.
Anent to this, we further recommended and Management agreed that the unused cash advances be refunded, and new cash advances be drawn as needed.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
require the Municipal Engineer to ensure that the projects are implemented  in accordance with the approved project implementation schedule and ADM guidelines; and
ensure that the projects’ documentary requirements as required in the DBM-DILG JMC 2017-3 be promptly prepared and completed before the receipt of ADM funds.
The Municipal Engineer presented the following problems encountered in the implementation of the ADM projects:  a). unaivailability of funds for counterpart from LGU, b). no data/records as to Deed of Donation to support the lot survey, c). absence of Road Right of Way Committee to handle the road lot survey.
We recommended and it was agreed that all necessary documentary and other requirements be complied prior to the formulation of project plans.
We recommended and the ICO-Municipal Treasurer agreed that the field collectors be strictly required to remit their collections to the Liquidating Officer intact and daily.

We recommended and the Head of the Inventory Committee agreed to:

prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Properties, Appraisal Report and other pertinent documents for the unserviceable properties identified in CY 2016; 
submit the related documents to COA for appraisal review; and
file an application for disposal within the year.
We recommended and the Supply Officer agreed to prepare and maintain Property Cards for all the property, plant and equipment owned by the Municipality using as reference the 2016 RPCPPE.

We recommended and Management agreed to fast track the construction of a municipal garage/motor pool which was recently started so the Municipality’s vehicles and heavy equipment will be protected against the elements of nature and safeguarded against damage due to vandalism and possible theft or cannibalization of parts.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to the following:
subsequent MOAs to be entered with NGOs strictly conform with the guidelines set  in Sections 4.5.3 and 4.5.8 of COA Circular No. 2007-001 and Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001;

Ang Pagtaltal sa Bala-an Bukid Foundation, Inc. be required to submit the liquidation reports and all other required documents for fund releases enumerated in COA Circular Nos. 2007-001 and 2012-001 and additional fund release not be granted unless prior fund releases were liquidated; and,

draw a JEV to correct the recording of the fund transfer.

We recommended and the BAC Chairperson agreed to ensure that bid tenders submitted by the bidders will include, among others, brand names of goods in the Technical Specifications. Also, upon award, the same information shall be embodied in the Contract of Agreement/Purchase Order between the Municipality and the winning bidder, as prescribed in COA Circular No. 96-010. Moreover, bid evaluation shall be in accordance with the procedures prescribed in Section 30 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended and the Supply Officer and Municipal Engineer committed to provide an appropriate storage space to serve as bodega for COA documents sufficient for the storage of ten years transactions.

We recommended that the wages of JERT members be charged to a separate administrative budget and only expenses relative to the conduct of disaster-related activities shall be charged to the LDRRMF in compliance with NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC 2013-1.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to disclose the information and details of the unexpended DRRMF in the Notes to the Financial Statements in accordance with the reporting guidelines set in COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002.

We recommended and the GAD Focal Person agreed to coordinate with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to establish a GAD database in the Municipality to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation. Likewise, The GAD focal person agreed to submit the Annual GPB to the DILG Regional Office for review and approval thru the Provincial Government and furnish the COA Audit Team the approved GPB. At the end of the year, Accomplishment Report shall likewise be furnished the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Head of the Inventory Committee agreed to gradually reconcile the differences in their records, effect the necessary adjustments if necessitated to facilitate presentation of the correct balance in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to exhaust all efforts to retrieve the records on the acquisition of these PPE. Otherwise, the Head of the Appraisal Committee agreed to create a schedule on the remaining useful lives of these assets so the Municipal Accountant could immediately provide depreciation to all depreciable assets, in accordance with the provisions of Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards.

We recommended and the Supply Officer agreed to prepare and maintain Property Cards for all the property, plant and equipment owned by the Municipality. Reconciliation between the records of the Supply Officer and those of the Municipal Accountant be undertaken regularly to ensure that receipts, custody, issuance, utilization, and disposal of all the PPE are properly reflected.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to demand liquidation reports from the barangays and Liga ng mga Barangay for the financial assistance granted, and henceforth, ensure that fund transfers be supported with Memorandum of Agreement and other pertinent documents required in COA Circular No. 2012-001 to ensure the diligent and judicious utilization of public funds.	
We further recommended that Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) shall be drawn to correct the entries made to record the previous releases of fund transfers.

We recommended and the Municipality agreed to the following:
That subsequent MOAs to be entered with NGOs shall conform with the guidelines set  in Sections 4.5.3 and 4.5.8 of COA Circular No. 2007-001 and Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001;
Ang Pagtaltal sa Bala-an Bukid Foundation, Inc. and other NGOs/POs be required to submit the liquidation reports and all other required documents for fund releases enumerated in COA Circular Nos. 2007-001 and 2012-001 and additional fund release should not be granted unless prior funds released were liquidated; and,
The Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entries to observe proper recording of the fund transfers to establish receivables and necessitate the liquidation of the fund releases.
We recommended that all projects identified under the 20% DF be fully implemented/completed during the year for the constituents to avail of their maximum benefits and utilization.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and MDRRM Officer agreed to ensure that the MDRRMF be spent solely for disaster-related activities as contemplated in the NDRRMC, DBM and DILG’s JMC No. 2013-1. Fuel expenses and wages of JERT members under the PMSK Program be charged against the funds of the office implementing the program.

We recommended and the GAD Focal Person, along with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer agreed to establish and maintain GAD Database for storage of necessary information in aid of effective gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.
We recommended and the ICO-Municipal Treasurer agreed that the field collectors be strictly required to remit their collections to the Liquidating Officer intact and daily.

We recommended and the Head of the Inventory Committee agreed to:
prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Properties, Appraisal Report and other pertinent documents for the unserviceable properties identified in CY 2016; and
submit the related documents to COA for appraisal review; and
file an application for disposal within the year.
We recommended and the Supply Officer agreed to prepare and maintain Property Cards for all the property, plant and equipment owned by the Municipality using as reference the 2016 RPCPPE.

We recommended and Management agreed to fast track the construction of a municipal garage/motor pool which was recently started so the Municipality’s vehicles and heavy equipment will be protected against the elements of nature and safeguarded against damage due to vandalism and possible theft or cannibalization of parts.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to the following:
subsequent MOAs to be entered with NGOs strictly conform with the guidelines set  in Sections 4.5.3 and 4.5.8 of COA Circular No. 2007-001 and Section 2.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001;
Ang Pagtaltal sa Bala-an Bukid Foundation, Inc. be required to submit the liquidation reports and all other required documents for fund releases enumerated in COA Circular Nos. 2007-001 and 2012-001 and additional fund release not be granted unless prior fund releases were liquidated; and,
draw a JEV to correct the recording of the fund transfer.

We recommended and the BAC Chairperson agreed to ensure that bid tenders submitted by the bidders will include, among others, brand names of goods in the Technical Specifications. Also, upon award, the same information shall be embodied in the Contract of Agreement/Purchase Order between the Municipality and the winning bidder, as prescribed in COA Circular No. 96-010. Moreover, bid evaluation shall be in accordance with the procedures prescribed in Section 30 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended and the Supply Officer and Municipal Engineer committed to provide an appropriate storage space to serve as bodega for COA documents sufficient for the storage of ten years transactions.

We recommended that the wages of JERT members be charged to a separate administrative budget and only expenses relative to the conduct of disaster-related activities shall be charged to the LDRRMF in compliance with NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC 2013-1.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to disclose the information and details of the unexpended DRRMF in the Notes to the Financial Statements in accordance with the reporting guidelines set in COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002.

We recommended and the GAD Focal Person agreed to coordinate with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to establish a GAD database in the Municipality to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation. Likewise, The GAD focal person agreed to submit the Annual GPB to the DILG Regional Office for review and approval thru the Provincial Government and furnish the COA Audit Team the approved GPB. At the end of the year, Accomplishment Report shall likewise be furnished the Office of the Auditor.
We recommended and the head of the Disposal Committee agreed to draw a timetable for the disposal of these unserviceable properties, and such disposal should not go beyond CY 2016.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to continue close monitoring of the cash advances and send demand letters to the accountable officers as soon as the purpose for which the cash advances were granted have been served, in accordance with the provisions of Sections 5.7 and 4.1.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. 

It was also recommended and agreed that no additional cash advances will be granted to the officials/employees pending liquidation or accounting of previous ones.

We recommended and the Sangguniang Bayan members agreed to expedite the revision and amendment of the Municipal Revenue Code and resubmit the same to the Sanggunian Panlalawigan for review and approval.  Provisions of Section 188 of RA 7160 on the publication should be strictly complied with.
observations.reason: The Revenue Code was revised and is presently at the legislative body for review. After which, the LGU will conduct a Public Hearing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to direct the office and field collectors and the liquidating officer to maintain cashbooks for collections and remittances per fund, and to update the same daily.

We recommended and the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant agreed to strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth in RA 7305 on the grant of hazard pay to the health employees. 
We also recommended the immediate refund of the excess hazard pay paid to the concerned personnel.

We recommended and the Local School Board agreed to comply with the pertinent provisions on the utilization of the SEF in the preparation of its Annual Budget, so that only the authorized projects be incorporated thereto; and to channel its available resources towards the implementation of priority projects enumerated in R.A. 7160 and DBM-DECS-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 1998.
